value.reason: The cited page is explicitly titled "Codex Security" and documents this exact capability — scanning repositories for security vulnerabilities using a repo-specific threat model, validating findings, and providing remediation guidance. It is clearly distinct from the adjacent capabilities (sandboxed execution, tool permissions, network/egress control, prompt-injection protection, dev-container integration), which the page explicitly redirects readers away from ("For Codex sandboxing, approvals, network controls, and admin settings, see Agent approvals & security"). The label "Codex Security" matches the page title and OpenAI's real product name. The description accurately reflects the page: it describes a security-review plugin and cloud service that scans repositories for vulnerabilities, validates findings, and delivers remediation guidance, integrating with Codex to audit code changes and agent-generated output — all of which are supported by the page content (plugin use cases include scanning repos, reviewing code changes, triaging backlogs, fixing/verifying findings; the cloud service uses a repo-specific threat model and validation). No invented claims are present.
